channel.title: MishMash Centre for AI and Creativity - Events
channel.description: Latest events from MishMash Centre for AI and Creativity

item.title: Concert: Quantum Loops, Broken Symmetries
item.description:
An evening exploring music with AI and quantum computing with Eduardo Reck Miranda and Victoria Johnson.
Artificial intelligence is revolutionising music creation and performance. AI-powered too...

item.title: MeshUp #1 – Teaching Machines to Move
item.description:
Postdoctoral researcher Benedikte Wallace (UiO) will present a short lecture on the topic Teaching Machines to Move for Human–AI Interaction.
Abstract
This talk looks at why expressive movement m...

item.title: MeshUp #2 – Prompting as Algorithmic Folklore
item.description:
Postdoctoral Researcher Marianne Gunderson (UiB) will present a short lecture on the topic Don’t Worry About Formalities: Prompting as Algorithmic Folklore.
Abstract
As large language models (LLM...

item.title: MeshUp #3 – MotionComposer and the Role of AI
item.description:
Robert Wechsler will present a short lecture on the topic MotionComposer – a device that turns movement into music. New Possibilities and the Role of AI.
Abstract
MotionComposer 3.0 is a device t...

item.title: MeshUp #4 – AI in the Music Classroom
item.description:
Olav Renolen Aasbø will present a short lecture on the topic Digitalization (and use of AI) in the music classroom.
Abstract
This session presents preliminary findings from an ongoing PhD project...

item.title: MeshUp #5 – Deepfake Deception and Human Resilience
item.description:
Sascha Frühholz will present a short lecture on the topic Human neurocognitive vulnerabilities and resilience against AI deepfake deception
Abstract
Deepfakes are AI-generated instances of human ...

item.title: MeshUp #6 – The Intellect of Sound
item.description:
Dániel Péter Biró will present a short lecture on the topic The Intellect of Sound: On Understanding and Translating Musical Parameters.
Abstract
Over the last 15 years I have worked in the field...

item.title: MeshUp #7 – AI-Driven Sports Highlights
item.description:
Pål Halvorsen will present a short lecture on the topic From Broadcast to Feed: AI-Driven Sports Highlight Production at Scale.
Abstract
Sports organizations and broadcasters face growing pressur...

item.title: MeshUp #8 – Klangen av Fremtiden?
item.description:
Anders Tveit will present a short lecture with the title Klangen av Fremtiden?.
Abstract
A short presentation featuring personal reflections and musical examples from my work with violinist Victo...

item.title: MeshUp #9 – Visual Literacy in AI Information Ecologies
item.description:
This week, Steven Bachelder will present a short talk titled: New tools of production -result in new information ecologies -requiring new forms of visual and information literacy.
Abstract
As Art...

item.title: RAVE Hackathon
item.description:
We are planning to run a mini-hackathon in connection with MishMash’s opening events. The main topic will be using RAVE models for realtime sound interaction and controlling them with biosignals.
...

item.title: Workshop on research application development
item.description:
Which research funding sources are available? How do you write a competitive grant? How can the MishMash network be leveraged in application processes?
Join us for a workshop on research applicati...

item.title: Work Package Meetups
item.description:
Meet fellow MishMashers for lunch, discussion and exchange.
Other MishMash events the same day
Check out all these exciting events on 8 April:
10:00-12:00: Workshop on research application de...

item.title: Det umuliges kunst: Panelsamtale om bruk av KI i kreativ praksis
item.description:
The arts face new challenges as AI-based tools become accessible to more people. How does this affect the creative process, and what dilemmas can arise?
We have invited artists, journalists, and r...

item.title: MishMash Opening Ceremony
item.description:
Welcome to the official opening of MishMash Centre for AI and Creativity, where we explore the role of AI in art and the potential of art in AI.
Program
Introductions
Rector Ragnhild Hennum...

item.title: MeshUp #10 – AI Meets Music Therapy
item.description:
This week, Felicity Baker will present a short talk titled MATCH: Where AI meets music therapy.
Abstract
Artificial intelligence offers new opportunities to deliver personalised, scalable interve...

item.title: WP4 Webinar #01: Critical AI and Creative Practice
item.description:
Welcome to a webinar organized by WP4 titled Critical AI and Creative Practice with Eryk Salvaggio.
Abstract
What does it mean to have a critical relationship with AI in creative work? In this we...

item.title: MeshUp #11 – Generative AI and Hyper-Abundance
item.description:
Terje Colbjørnsen will give a short talk titled Generative AI and streaming in the creative industries: Toward hyper-abundance.
Abstract
The impact of digital technologies on creative industries ...

item.title: MeshUp #12 – AI and the Future of Sámi Yoik
item.description:
This week, Camilla Brattland will give a short talk titled Enabling a prosperous future for Sámi yoik – with AI?.
Abstract This talk relates to the RQ6.2: How can AI technologies be used ethically...

item.title: MeshUp #13 – Evaluating and Comparing AI Models
item.description:
This week, Changkyu Choi will present on the topic How AI conferences evaluate and compare models.
Abstract This talk provides an overview of the current landscape of how AI models are evaluated a...

item.title: MeshUp #14 – AI-Assisted Instrument Creation
item.description: Join us for Meshup 14, our weekly gathering for MishMashers. This week, Øyvind Brandtsegg will present on the topic Instrument creation on the border of accelerated creativity – Physical interfaces...

item.title: MeshUp #15 – Human-Machine Musical Dialogues
item.description:
Örjan de Manzano and Oded Ben-Tal will give the short talk Creative Musical Dialogues Between Human and Machine: A Novel Approach to Studying Improvisation and Joint Action.
Abstract
Recent advan...

item.title: MeshUp #16 – AI and the Material Footprint of Cultural Heritage
item.description:
This week, Eivind Røssaak will talk about When the Memory of a Nation Goes Mineral: AI, Nanofabrication, and the Material Footprint of Cultural Heritage Research.
Abstract
Recent breakthroughs in...

item.title: MeshUp #17 – Weekly MishMash Gathering
item.description:
Hanne Maren Meldahl from Western Norway University of Applied Sciences will present Co-Creating with AI - Pedagogical Innovation and Postdigital Practices in Online Creative Collaboration.
Abstrac...

item.title: WP4 Webinar #02: Young people's practices with Generative AI: Emerging relationships of (dis)trust
item.description:
Welcome to a webinar organized by WP4 titled Young people’s practices with Generative AI: Emerging relationships of (dis)trust with Fride Haram Klykken.
Abstract GenAI has become widely accessible...

item.title: MeshUp #18 – Copyright, Street Art and AI
item.description:
Siri-Helen Egeland will present on the topic of Copyright, street art and AI.
Abstract
Street artists are on the forefront of both the use and the consequences of text-to image generators. The ar...

item.title: WP1 Research Webinar: NCA for Artistic Exploration
item.description:
Welcome to a webinar organized by WP1 titled NCAs for Artistic Exploration with Etienne Guichard.
Abstract Etienne’s Presentation will cover Neural Cellular Automata (NCAs), their high-level appli...

item.title: MeshUp #19 – Neck-Deep in Digital Oil? Public Broadcasting Archives as AI Training Data Sets
item.description:
Brecht Declercq will present Neck-Deep in Digital Oil? Public Broadcasting Archives as AI Training Data Sets.
Abstract
The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence (AI) has triggered an unprece...

MishMash MeetUps are short, informal meetings in the consortium where both early career and established researchers present ongoing projects....

item.description: The MishMash opening conference showcases frameworks and examples based on the three approaches of the centre. It features discussions between researchers from multiple backgrounds, from the perspe...

item.title: AR@K27 — 9th Annual Artistic Research Conference
item.description:
Save the date.
AR@K27 — the 9th Annual Artistic Research Conference — takes place on 13–14 April 2027 at Kristiania University of Applied Sciences, Oslo.
The conference is organised in collaborat...
